description: Lipoic acid (also known as 6,8-thioctic acid, 1,2-dithiolane-3-pentanoic acid) is a naturally occurring cofactor and potent antioxidant. It is an essential cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase, α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase, and glycine cleavage enzyme complexes. It is used in therapies for diseases accompanied by oxidative stress such as diabetes, atherosclerosis, neurodegenerative diseases, and AIDS, and as a detoxication agent for heavy metal poisoning.
tags: antioxidant; cofactor; therapeutic; detoxication agent
